localPsalter.storageModel: consolidated Psalm-level records with translation strings under record.text

policyFindings: Rotherham Psalms source is ordinary MT/Hebrew Psalm scope, Psalms 1-150.
The app psalter is a consolidated 155-record psalter and includes extended Orthodox material in Psalms 151-155.
Existing local Psalm records already allow translation-specific verse marker counts within the same Psalm-level record.
NRSV/Coverdale mismatch alone is not a safe blocker because KJV/JPS1985 often use a different marker count in the same local record.
Rotherham should not be imported into Psalms 151-155 from this source.
A bounded importer may add text.Rotherham to Psalms 1-150 only, preserving the consolidated Psalm-level string schema.

recommendedImporterGuard.format: Assemble each Rotherham Psalm as newline-delimited 'Psalm:Verse text' strings under record.text.Rotherham.

1.preview: How happy the man, who hath not walked in the counsel of the lawless,—and, in the way of sinners, ha...
But, in the law of Yahweh, is his delight,—and, in his law, doth he talk with himself day and night.
So doth he become like a tree planted by streams of waters,—that yieldeth, its fruit, in its season,...
Not so, the lawless,—but as chaff which is driven about by the wind:

2.preview: Wherefore have nations assembled in tumult? Or should, peoples, mutter an empty thing?
The kings of earth take their station, and, grave men, have met by appointment together,—against Yah...
Let us break asunder their bonds,—and cast from us their cords!
He that sitteth in the heavens, will laugh,—My Lord, will mock at them:

22.preview: My GOD, my GOD, why hast thou forsaken me? Far from saving me, The words of my loud lamentation?
My God! I keep crying—By day, and thou dost not answer, and, By night, and there is no rest for me.
But, thou, art holy, Who inhabitest the praises of Israel.
In thee, trusted our fathers, They trusted, and thou didst deliver them;

55.preview: Give ear, O God, to my prayer, And do not hide thyself from my supplication:
Attend unto me, and answer me, I may ramble in my complaining, And be driven to and fro—
Because of the noise of the enemy, By reason of the oppression of the lawless one, For they would le...
My heart, would writhe within me, And, the terrors of Death, have fallen upon me:

66.preview: Make a joyful noise unto God, all the earth;
Praise ye in song the glory of his Name, Celebrate the glory of his praise:
Say unto God—How fearful in thy doings, Through the abounding of thy power, shall thy foes come crin...
All the earth, shall bow themselves down to thee, And sing praises unto thee,. Shall praise in song ...

150.preview: Praise ye Yah, Praise ye GOD in his sanctuary, Praise him, in his strong expanse:
Praise him, for his mighty deeds, Praise him, according to his exceeding greatness:
Praise him, with the blast of a horn, Praise him, with the harp and lyre:
Praise him, with timbrel and dance,—Praise him, with stringed instrument and flute,
